directiveText: When OpenAI surfaces in a technical validation, lead with a long-context and tool-use reliability eval on the prospect's own data, plus our zero-data-retention enterprise terms. Do not anchor the conversation on per-token price — we lose that frame.

Every technology deal over $1M must surface the SOC 2 Type II report and the zero-retention DPA proactively during technical validation, before the security review starts. Reactive security packets are our number-one cause of slipped close dates this quarter.

Capture the decision_process and paper_process MEDDPICC dimensions explicitly by the end of every technical validation call. 'We don't know who signs' at proposal stage is a forecasting failure, not a surprise.
For AI-native prospects, get the CTO or head of engineering into the technical validation, not just a champion engineer. Champion-only deals stall the moment they hit a security or budget gate.

Any rate-limit or capacity-at-scale question gets a Solutions Architect and a concrete throughput and SLA plan within 24 hours. Do not hand-wave capacity — it reads as 'they can't actually serve us at scale,' and that is exactly the doubt OpenAI plants.

When a prospect is also evaluating Google Gemini or Vertex, contrast on enterprise data governance, model steerability, and cross-cloud portability — not public benchmark scores. The benchmark conversation is a trap that favors whoever published last.

Log every named-competitor mention as a signal, even when you think the deal is safe. Coordinator pattern detection across the portfolio is only as good as the completeness of the competitive data each rep captures.
Deals over $2M require Marcus and Deal Desk on the negotiation call. No exceptions — single-threaded negotiations at this size are where we give away margin.
